TRANSCRIPT
Book Launch Bonus – Goals
TRANSCRIPT
We're going to start talking about book launches in the same place we started talking
about regular launches back in module one of Product Launch Formula and that's with goals
because unless you know what you want out of your book launch you can't really design the
launch properly to get you where you want to go. Just like with a regular launch for a product
your strategy is going to be dictated by what you want to get out of it. There's the saying, "If you
don't know where you're going any road will take you there." The thing is if you take any road
you're not going to get where you want to go.
That's why you need to design a strategy around your goals. The question
is why are you writing a book? What do you want to accomplish with that book?
Just getting a book done, that's a big accomplishment but it's better if it gets you somewhere, if
it gets you where you want to go. Your answer to why you're writing the book and
what you want to accomplish will drive your launch strategy. For example, my goals
with my launch, well these are my primary goals: I wanted the additional positioning authority
of being an author, specifically a best-selling author, specifically a New York Times best-selling
author.
That would give me additional positioning and authority because sort of where I'm going
in my career is this ever bigger arc and ever bigger impact in sort of breaking out of this online
marketing world into the larger business world. I wanted that additional positioning and
authority. I also wanted a new lead source. I wanted a new way to bring leads into my business.
The book was very much about getting awareness outside of my smaller market and getting
awareness out in the bigger world. It was about legacy frankly. I hope this doesn't sound too ego-
full but I created something really cool.
Product Launch Formula has literally changed the way stuff is sold online. It's changed
the marketing. My friend Eben Pagan said it's one of the most important marketing innovations
in the last one hundred years. I don't know if I would go that far but it is a pretty big deal. It's
really changed marketing. It's changed the way stuff is sold. It's changed the way people are able
to get their message out into the world. People went from where now people with real expertise
or knowledge, they're able to build crazy big impactful businesses in a very short period of time
where they weren't able to do that before.
Since I created this thing I figure, well someday when they're writing the history books
someone is going to get credit for this. I figure since it was mine, I created it, I invented it, I
might as well be the one that gets the credit for it. In one way writing this book was sort of the
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

TRANSCRIPT
Book Launch Bonus – Goals
way to put this out into the world that yes, this is my baby. This is my creation. There was a part
of legacy here as well. I also just wanted to be able to make a bigger impact. A lot of people can't
afford Product Launch Formula so this is sort of the way to get the Product Launch Formula in
my work out for the masses in a way that lots of people could get.
One thing that you want to note is that making money was not on my list here. This
wasn't about making money or at least money initially. Of course, a new lead source was going to
be a way for me to make money long term but it wasn't like I was writing a book and planning on
selling a lot of books and making a lot of money from book royalties. Those were my overall
goals for the book. That led to my goals for the book launch…I wanted to have it be a New York
Times bestseller. I wanted to be a New York Times bestseller. I wanted to be on the bestseller list
and hopefully I wanted to be on the New York Times list for more than a single week.
I wanted to be there for more than one week because then I thought it could develop
some legs and start to develop some real gravity and start to sell on its own beyond my
promotions. I also wanted to make money for my partners. I knew that I was going to be using
my partners in this launch so I wanted to be able to…I wanted to make money for them. Any
time our partners are involved I want to make money for my partners, and I wanted to have the
overall process be profitable. Frankly, if I got those first three goals and it wasn't profitable and I
lost some money on the initial thing I knew that if I achieved those top three goals then in the
long run I would make money because I would start to generate those additional leads.
All these were based on me having some really strong assets in my business. I just didn't
wake up one morning and said, "I want to be a New York Times bestseller." Just pie in the sky
that's a cool goal to have. I looked at this whole process and I looked at what it would take and I
knew that I had assets that would support this kind of launch. The question is what are your
goals for your book? It could be that you just always wanted to write a book. It could be you
want to make a lot of money from your book. It could be like me you want positioning, you want
to be a bestselling author, that you want lead generation, you want a new way for people to find
you to come into your business, to come into your world. It could be legacy sort of like mine.
It could just be that you want to help lots and lots of people or it could just be that you've
got this inside you and you want to get your art out into the world. It might be all of those, it
might be some of those, it might just be one of those. If it's just money then I will warn you that I
don't necessarily think that you know, if you're just sort of getting in this world of this expert's
world, this guru world, this teaching world, this information world, this knowledge
entrepreneurial world then there's a lot of ways to package up your expertise.

TRANSCRIPT
Book Launch Bonus – Goals
If the book is the first thing you're doing it's probably not going to make you as much
money as if you took that same knowledge and you built it into a knowledge product. If you built
it into an online product, if you turn it into an online training, if you turn it into a live event, if
you turn it into a webinar, if you did a seed launch with it. Books can build your business but
rarely very, very, very few people make a substantial amount of money making a living wage just
from publishing books especially if it's just one book.
If it's just money that you're after out of this book and you think you're going to make a
lot of money from royalties, a lot of money from selling the book, then there probably are better
ways to do that with the same knowledge. That's just an overview of some possible goals. I'm
sure I've left some off the list here. You might have some goals that aren't on the list here but
there are some of the big ones. It's important that you know what your goals are
before you start your launch process.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
